Metadata.ExomizerCommand: exomizer sfx 0x100C "FRANKENSTEINJNR-0C00-100C" -Di_irq_exit=0 -s "inc $d030 lsr $d011" -f "w1: bit $d011 bpl w1 w2: bit $d011 bmi w2 jsr $fda3 rol $d011 jsr $0c00 dec $d030" -x "inc $d020" -o "Frankenstein Jnr.prg"
Metadata.Notes:
Added an init routine at $0C00 which copies 512 bytes of data from $0C20 down to $0200 so that the dump can start at $0400. It then jumps to the entry point at $100C (it couldn't use RTS because of the copy overwriting the boot code).
Note: as the original game is frozen, the breakpoint to use for dumping is actually $6BBB.
This also means that a small modification was required to prevent the startup code located at $100C from overwriting some memory with data which had changed by the time the freeze was taken.
The change was to NOP out the JSR $1134 at $1030, as the data was already present at the right locations in the dump.
And finally, the loading screen is from the Spectrum version
